description: Fix the 3 remaining OPEN issues from FULL_ARCHITECTURE_MAP.md section 6: (1) Rename billing.ts subscriptions DB table from 'subscriptions' to 'service_subscriptions' to avoid collision with infra.ts subscriptions table, (2) Consolidate legacy tag tables (contact_tags, lead_tags) by marking them deprecated and having code comments point to entity_tags, (3) Add caching to the leaderboards API using the existing withCache pattern from lib/cache/redis.ts. Finally update FULL_ARCHITECTURE_MAP.md section 6 to mark these three items as RESOLVED.
steps: Step 1 - Fix subscriptions table name collision: In drizzle/schema/billing.ts, change pgTable('subscriptions', ...) to pgTable('service_subscriptions', ...) on the serviceSubscriptions export (line 279). This renames the actual DB table to 'service_subscriptions' so it no longer collides with the infra.ts subscriptions table (which remains as 'subscriptions' for platform billing). Update drizzle/schema/_registry.ts metadata if it references the old table name (around line 2594, the name field already says 'service_subscriptions' so it may already be correct).

Step 2 - Fix subscriptions index names: In drizzle/schema/billing.ts, the serviceSubscriptions table has indexes like 'idx_subscriptions_contact', 'idx_subscriptions_company', 'idx_subscriptions_status'. Rename these to 'idx_service_subscriptions_contact', 'idx_service_subscriptions_company', 'idx_service_subscriptions_status' to avoid index name collisions with infra.ts subscriptions indexes.

Step 3 - Consolidate legacy tag tables: In drizzle/schema/crm.ts, add deprecation JSDoc comments to the contactTags and leadTags table definitions (around lines 465-479) explaining that these are legacy tables maintained for backward compatibility and that new code should use entity_tags with entityType='contact' or entityType='lead'. Do NOT remove them (backward compat).

Step 4 - Add caching to leaderboards API: In app/api/tenant/leaderboards/route.ts, import withCache from '@/lib/cache/redis' and wrap the database query execution in a withCache call. The cache key should include the tenantId, metric, period, and any custom start/end dates. Use a TTL of 300 seconds (5 minutes). The cache key pattern should be: `leaderboard:${ctx.tenantId}:${metric}:${period}:${startDate.toISOString()}:${endDate.toISOString()}`.

Step 5 - Update FULL_ARCHITECTURE_MAP.md: In section 6.1, change the subscriptions table collision row status from '**[OPEN]** Requires broader migration planning to rename one table' to '**[RESOLVED]** billing.ts table renamed to service_subscriptions'. In section 6.6, change the 'Duplicate table name collision' row status to '**[RESOLVED]** billing.ts pgTable renamed from subscriptions to service_subscriptions'. Change the 'Legacy vs systematic tagging' row status to '**[RESOLVED]** Legacy tables deprecated with JSDoc; new code uses entity_tags'. In section 6.8, change the 'Leaderboards page calculation' row status to '**[RESOLVED]** 5-minute TTL cache added via withCache'. In section 6.9, update items 4 and 7 to mark them RESOLVED. Also add a note about the leaderboard caching being resolved.

Step 6 - Add a unit test: Create tests/unit/leaderboard-cache.test.ts that verifies the caching logic is properly integrated (mocking the cache module and DB). Follow the vitest patterns from other test files in the project.
acceptance_criteria: drizzle/schema/billing.ts serviceSubscriptions uses pgTable('service_subscriptions', ...) instead of pgTable('subscriptions', ...)
No index name collisions between billing.ts and infra.ts subscription tables
drizzle/schema/crm.ts contactTags and leadTags have @deprecated JSDoc comments directing to entity_tags
app/api/tenant/leaderboards/route.ts uses withCache with a 5-minute TTL
FULL_ARCHITECTURE_MAP.md section 6 marks all three issues as RESOLVED
findings: All changes verified: tsc --noEmit passes, vitest run passes (435 tests, 38 files). The withCache import and usage in leaderboards compiles correctly. The billing.ts table rename does not affect any other imports since the export name (serviceSubscriptions) is unchanged.
